meaning: adj.长着软毛的,穿毛皮的,毛皮制的
examples: But inspection of furred-up pipes and recalculations to make the books balance can take months.
My student tried hard to get the thick furred look of the blue area, but all he could manage was a flaccid, watery pool of blue.
For centuries, scientists have catalogued the creatures of Europe - the tall and the small, the feathered and the furred.
